w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: FITOSYS
The System Behind the Result
Brand Identity Document
Coach-Focused Edition
Version 2.0 | 2026 | Internal Use Only
1. Brand Overview

Independent fitness coaches in India lose an average of 2 to 3 clients every month — not because their coaching is poor, but because there is no system catching renewals, following up on check-ins, or confirming payments before a client quietly disappears. At
8,000 per client per month, that is
2,88,000 in preventable annual revenue loss. For most coaches, that is 2 to 4 months of income, gone.
Fitosys was built to stop that.

We are a business operating system built specifically for independent fitness, wellness, and yoga coaches. Not a workout app. Not a generic CRM. A purpose-built platform that automates the three tasks draining a coach's time and revenue every single week — client onboarding with payment, weekly WhatsApp check-ins, and program renewal reminders.
Coaches using Fitosys do not chase clients for payments. They do not spend Sunday evenings manually sending check-in messages to 30 people. They do not lose track of
who is about to churn. The system handles all of it, on WhatsApp, in the background, while the coach focuses on coaching.
The result is not just time saved. It is revenue protected, client relationships maintained, and a business that runs with the same discipline the coach brings to every session.
What Fitosys Is

A SaaS platform for independent coaches that automates client onboarding, weekly check-ins, and renewal reminders — delivered natively on WhatsApp, powered by AI, and built for the Indian coaching market.
What Fitosys Is Not
A fitness app for end consumers
A workout builder or nutrition tracker
A marketplace or gym management tool
A generic CRM adapted for coaching
A Western platform with an Indian price tag
Who It Is For

Independent fitness coaches, yoga instructors, wellness practitioners, and nutrition coaches managing 15 to 50 active clients, operating in India, and losing hours every week to admin work that a system should be doing for them.
2. About Us
Here is what coaches tell us when we ask why they built their business.
"I became a coach to help people live healthier lives. Not to chase invoices and update spreadsheets."
And here is what their clients tell us when we ask why they left.
"I felt like my coach didn't really know how I was doing. Outside the session, I was on my own."
Same business. Two people. Completely opposite experiences of the same problem.

The coach is not neglecting their clients. They are buried. Thirty WhatsApp threads, manual payment follow-ups, renewal reminders written from memory, Sunday evenings lost to check-in messages that should have gone out at 7 PM but went out at 11. By the time a client starts to disengage, the coach is too stretched to catch the signal. By the time the renewal comes around, the relationship has already cooled.
The client does not see any of this. They just feel the silence.
This is the gap Fitosys was built to close.

Not by making coaches work harder. By building the system that runs underneath the coaching relationship — so every client gets checked in every week without the coach manually sending a single message, every renewal gets caught before it slips, and every payment gets collected without a single awkward follow-up conversation.
The coach shows up as attentive, consistent, and organised — because the system is doing the operational work that was previously making that impossible.
Good coaches are losing good clients right now. Not because the coaching is poor. Because the infrastructure holding the relationship together does not exist.
Fitosys is that infrastructure.
3. Brand Pillars
Every product decision, every feature, every piece of communication is measured against these four pillars. They are not aspirational statements. They are operating principles.
Relationship First

Every feature Fitosys builds starts with one question: does this make the coach-client relationship stronger? Automated check-ins exist not to save the coach time — though they do — but to ensure every client hears from their coach every single
week without fail. Consistency builds trust. Trust drives retention. Retention protects revenue. The relationship is not a soft metric. It is the business.
In practice:
Check-in messages go out in the coach's name, from the coach's number, every week — whether the coach is with another client, travelling, or asleep.
Time Is the Product

A coach's only non-renewable resource is time. Every hour spent on WhatsApp admin, manual payment follow-ups, and renewal tracking is an hour not spent coaching, resting, or growing the business. Fitosys is built with an obsessive focus on returning that time — not by cutting corners on client experience, but by automating every task that does not require a human coach to do it.
In practice:
Coaches using Fitosys get 2 to 3 hours back every week. That is one additional client slot, every single week.
Intelligence Over Instinct

A coach managing 30 clients cannot hold 30 accurate mental models simultaneously. Important signals — a client's energy dropping over three consecutive weeks, a pattern of missed sessions before a churn — get lost in the noise of daily coaching. Fitosys surfaces these signals automatically, giving the coach a clear view of their entire client base every Monday morning so they can act on data, not gut feel.
In practice:
The weekly AI summary tells the coach exactly who needs attention, who is excelling, and what to prioritise — in under 2 minutes.
Systems Create Professionalism

A coach who onboards clients through a clean payment page, sends structured weekly check-ins, and follows up on renewals automatically does not just operate more efficiently — they are perceived differently by their clients. Professionalism is not about aesthetics. It is about consistency. Fitosys gives every independent coach the operational consistency of a much larger organisation, without the overhead.
In practice:
From the first payment to the last renewal reminder, every client touchpoint is structured, timely, and on-brand for the coach.
4. Vision Statement
To be the operating system every independent fitness coach in India runs their business on —
where no coach loses a client to poor follow-up, and no coaching relationship ends because the admin got in the way.
What This Means in Practice
We measure success by how much revenue coaches stop losing, not just how many coaches sign up
Trust is earned by being invisible — the system works so reliably that coaches forget it is there
We build long-term coaching businesses, not just short-term efficiency gains
5. Mission Statement
To give every independent fitness, wellness, and yoga coach in India the operational infrastructure
they were never given — so they can spend their time coaching, not managing, and grow a business
that runs as well as the programmes they deliver.
Mission Breakdown
What We Do
Why It Matters
Automate client onboarding and payment
Coaches stop losing clients to friction at the first step
Run weekly WhatsApp check-ins automatically
Every client feels heard, every week, without the coach lifting a finger
Catch renewals before they lapse
Revenue that was always earned stops being lost to poor follow-up
Surface AI coaching intelligence weekly
Coaches act on signals, not gut feel — and catch at-risk clients early
6. Core Values
These are not fitness culture values. They are the values of a software company that coaches trust with their business.
01 — The Coach Comes First. Always.

Every product decision, every feature prioritisation, every support interaction starts with one question: does this make a coach's working day better? Not easier for us to build. Not impressive on a features list. Better for the coach running a real business with real clients depending on them.
In practice: If a feature saves us engineering time but adds friction for a coach, we do not ship it.
02 — Reliability Is Non-Negotiable

A coach cannot afford for the system to fail on Sunday evening when check-ins are due, or on the day a renewal reminder needs to go out. Fitosys is infrastructure. Infrastructure has to work every time, without the coach checking whether it did.
In practice: When something breaks, we fix it before the coach notices. When we cannot, we tell them immediately and own the failure completely.
03 — Simplicity Is Respect

An independent coach is not a software engineer. Every minute spent figuring out how to use Fitosys is a minute stolen from coaching. We build simple because we respect the coach's time too much to make them learn a complex tool.
In practice: If a new coach cannot complete their first setup in under 30 minutes without help, the product is not simple enough yet.
04 — Data Should Tell the Coach Something Useful

Dashboards full of metrics that do not change a coach's behaviour are decoration. Every piece of data Fitosys surfaces exists because a coach can act on it. If they cannot act on it, we do not show it.
In practice: The Monday morning AI summary delivers one specific priority for the coach to act on that week. Not ten insights. One.
05 — Build for the Coach in Front of You

The independent Indian fitness coach is not a scaled-down version of a Western gym chain. They work differently, communicate differently, price differently, and have clients who behave differently. Fitosys is built for the actual coach in front of us — not a theoretical global user that does not exist yet.
In practice: WhatsApp is the primary communication channel because that is where Indian coaches and their clients already are. We go to them. They do not come to us.
06 — Grow With the Coaches Who Trust Us

The coaches who sign up early are not just customers. They are the product co-builders. Their feedback shapes what gets built next, their struggles expose what is broken, and their growth validates whether the platform is doing its job.
In practice: When a coach tells us something is not working, that conversation goes directly into the next sprint. Not a backlog. The next sprint.
7. Target Audience

Fitosys is not built for every coach in India. It is built for the independent coach who is good at what they do, has started building a client base, and has hit the moment where the tools they stitched together are no longer enough — but a complex enterprise platform is complete overkill.
MVP Target: Yoga, wellness, and nutrition coaches. 12 to 20 active clients. Currently using Google Forms, Sheets, and WhatsApp. Feeling the system start to break.
Persona 1 — Meera, The Yoga Instructor
"I have 18 clients. I love every single session. I dread every Sunday."
Profile
Age 27 to 38. Mumbai, Pune, or Bangalore. Solo yoga practice — individual and small group sessions, mix of online and in-person. Built her client base through Instagram and word of mouth.
Revenue
80,000/month. Charges per session or monthly packages. Renewals are informal — a WhatsApp message when she remembers, or when the client asks.
Current Setup
Google Form for intake. WhatsApp chats for check-ins. Excel sheet she updates inconsistently. UPI payment links sent manually with awkward follow-ups.
Her Real Pain

She did not become a yoga instructor to manage a spreadsheet. The admin makes her feel like she is winging it. She has lost two clients in six months not because the yoga was poor but because she lost track of them between sessions.
What She Needs
Automated weekly check-ins. Renewal reminders that go out without her touching them. A clean onboarding link that collects everything and takes payment in one step.
Switching Trigger
Proof that it works on WhatsApp without her clients needing to download anything. She has tried getting clients onto apps before. They do not use them.
Persona 2 — Ananya, The Wellness Coach
"My clients trust me with things they do not tell their families. I cannot afford to look disorganised."
Profile

Age 30 to 42. Delhi, Hyderabad, or fully online. Certified in stress management, mindfulness, or holistic health. Clients are urban professionals dealing with burnout, anxiety, or lifestyle health issues.
Revenue
1,20,000/month. Premium pricing per session or 8 to 12 week programs. Clients pay more and expect more — a higher standard of communication and follow-through.
Current Setup
Google Forms for intake, basic client tracker, individual check-in voice notes or messages. As her base has grown from 8 to 18 clients, the manual effort has scaled unsustainably.
Her Real Pain
Professionalism is everything in her niche. Clients paying
15,000/month expect a premium experience. A manual, inconsistent follow-up process undermines the trust she has built in the session room.
What She Needs
A professional client experience from first payment to weekly check-in. AI summaries that surface early disengagement signals. A renewal flow that feels like a natural next step, not an invoice.
Switching Trigger
Seeing that check-in questions can be customised for wellness. "How many sessions did you complete" is irrelevant to her clients. "How would you rate your stress levels this week" is not.
Persona 3 — Riya, The Nutrition Coach
"I spend more time explaining payment options than I do talking about nutrition."
Profile
Age 25 to 35. Fully online. Certified nutritionist running individual coaching programs, typically 8 to 16 weeks. Builds pipeline through Instagram content and referrals. 12 to 22 active clients.
Revenue
75,000/month. Program-based pricing — clients pay upfront for a fixed duration. The challenge is not getting clients to pay once. It is getting them to renew when the program ends.
Current Setup
UPI or bank transfer with manual confirmation. Google Form at program start. WhatsApp check-ins individually. No reliable system for tracking who is mid-program, ending soon, or gone quiet.
Her Real Pain
Renewal conversion is lower than it should be — not because her results are poor but because the renewal conversation happens too late, too awkwardly, and without data to anchor it.
What She Needs

One onboarding link that handles intake and Razorpay payment together. Automated renewal reminders with the client's own progress data included. A dashboard showing who is in which week of their program.
Switching Trigger
Seeing the renewal reminder template — specifically that it includes the client's check-in data and progress summary. That context makes the renewal feel earned rather than chased.
8. Unique Selling Proposition
USP in One Line:
Fitosys gives independent coaches 2 to 3 hours back every week by automating the client management work that WhatsApp and spreadsheets were never built to handle.
The Full USP
Your coaching is not the problem. The time it takes to run your coaching business is.

Every independent coach we have spoken to is good at what they do. The yoga instructor who holds 18 clients with genuine care. The wellness coach whose clients trust her with things they do not share with their families. The nutrition coach whose results speak for themselves. None of them have a coaching problem.
They have a time problem.

Three hours every Sunday sending check-in messages manually. Forty minutes mid-month chasing a payment that should have been collected automatically. A renewal conversation that happens too late because there was no system to trigger it at the right moment. An intake process that takes four WhatsApp exchanges to complete what should take one link and two minutes.

Add it up across a month and the average independent coach with 20 clients is spending 12 to 15 hours on tasks that have nothing to do with coaching. That is two full working days every month spent on admin that a system should be handling.
Fitosys gives those hours back.

Not by replacing the coach. Not by putting clients through a cold automated experience that feels nothing like the relationship they signed up for. By running the operational layer underneath the coaching relationship — so check-ins go out every week in the coach's name, renewals get caught before they lapse, and every Monday morning the coach knows exactly which clients need their attention.
The Single Differentiator

Everything works on WhatsApp. No app for clients to download. No new platform for them to log into. No behaviour change required from anyone. The coach's clients are already on WhatsApp. Fitosys meets them there — and makes the coach look like they have a team running their business, even when it is just them.
Why Every Other Tool Failed
What coaches have tried
Why it did not work
WhatsApp Business
No automation, no scheduling, no payment collection, no renewal tracking
Google Forms + Sheets
Manual data entry, no triggers, no WhatsApp integration, breaks at 20+ clients
Trainerize / My PT Hub
Built for Western gym workflows, requires client app download, priced in USD
Generic CRMs
Not built for coaching, requires significant setup, no WhatsApp native delivery
Doing nothing
Works until it does not — stops working at exactly the moment a coach is trying to grow
Fitosys is the only platform built specifically for Indian yoga, wellness, and nutrition coaches
that automates client management natively on WhatsApp — with no app required from the client
and no complex setup required from the coach.
9. Brand Personality

Fitosys sounds like the most organised person in the room who also genuinely understands what it means to run a coaching business alone. Not a tech company talking down to coaches. Not a fitness brand hyping them up. A straight-talking operator who has seen the problem clearly, built the solution carefully, and communicates with the coach the way a trusted business partner would.
Four Words That Define the Voice
Clear
Every message, every screen, every instruction is written so a coach understands it immediately without reading it twice. Clarity is not dumbing down. It is respect for the reader's time.
Grounded

Fitosys does not overpromise. It states what the product does, what the coach can expect, and what it will not do. Coaches have been burned by overblown promises before. Fitosys earns trust by underselling and overdelivering.
Warm but not soft

There is genuine care for the coach behind every product decision. That care shows in the writing — but it never tips into cheerleading or empty encouragement. The warmth is in the specificity. Knowing Meera dreads Sunday evenings is warmer than telling her she is amazing.
Precise

Fitosys uses numbers when it has them, names the specific pain instead of the general category, and avoids vague benefit language. "Saves you time" is vague. "Gets your Sunday evening back" is precise. Precision builds credibility faster than any adjective.
Voice in Practice
Situation
Wrong tone
Fitosys tone
Landing page headline
Transform your coaching business today
Stop losing clients to missed follow-ups.
Onboarding welcome
Welcome to the Fitosys family! So excited to have you
Your account is set up. Here is what happens next.
Feature description
Powerful AI-driven insights to supercharge your coaching
Every Monday morning, you get a summary of which clients need your attention that week.
Renewal reminder
Don't let your hard work go to waste — renew now!
Your 12-week program ends in 7 days. 18 sessions completed. Avg energy 7.2/10.
Error message
Oops! Something went wrong. Please try again
Message not delivered. We will retry in 10 minutes.
Empty state
Looks quiet here! Add your first client to get started
No active clients yet. Share your onboarding link to get your first client enrolled.
What Fitosys Never Sounds Like
Hype — no "revolutionary", "game-changing", "supercharge", or "crush it"
Vague — "better client relationships" means nothing. Name the specific thing.
Apologetic — Fitosys does not hedge its value with "we hope" or "this might help"
Corporate — no passive voice, no committee-written sentences
Motivational without substance — no "You've got this" or "Keep pushing"
10. Key Messaging
These are the approved lines for every Fitosys communication surface. Use them verbatim where possible. Never drift from the underlying idea.
Master Tagline
"The system behind the result."

Replaces "Where Systems Meet Strength" which was written for a fitness consumer. This tagline acknowledges what the coach does — delivers results — and positions Fitosys as the operational layer that makes it sustainable.
Primary Headline
"Stop losing clients to missed follow-ups."
Use this as the hero headline on the landing page, the opening line of any cold outreach, and the first slide of any pitch deck.
Supporting Headlines by Persona
Persona
Headline
Yoga coaches
Your clients trust you with their health. Give them a system that matches that standard.
Wellness coaches
Your renewal rate should reflect your results. Right now it reflects your follow-up system. Let's fix that.
Nutrition coaches
Built for Indian coaches. Works on WhatsApp. No app required from your clients.
Manual WhatsApp users
WhatsApp was not built to run a coaching business. Fitosys was.
Elevator Pitch — 30 Seconds
Use this in LinkedIn DMs, introductory calls, and referral conversations.

"Fitosys is a business management platform for independent yoga, wellness, and nutrition coaches. It automates the three things that take the most time every week — client onboarding with payment, weekly WhatsApp check-ins, and renewal reminders. Coaches get 2 to 3 hours back every week and stop losing clients to missed follow-ups. Everything works on WhatsApp so clients do not need to download anything. It takes about 30 minutes to set up and runs automatically after that."
Proof Points
2 to 3 hours back every week. Every week.
Every client checked in, every Sunday, automatically.
Renewal reminders fire at T-7 days with the client's own progress data — energy scores, sessions completed, program end date.
One onboarding link. Client fills the form, selects a program, pays via Razorpay — UPI, card, net banking. Done in under 5 minutes.
No app download required from clients. Everything works on WhatsApp.
Set up in 30 minutes. Runs automatically after that.
Built for yoga, wellness, and nutrition coaches in India. Not adapted from a Western gym tool.
Objection Responses
"My clients won't respond to automated messages."

The messages go out from your WhatsApp number, in your name, with your client's first name. To your client it looks and feels like a message from you. The only difference is you did not spend Sunday evening sending 20 of them.
"I already use WhatsApp Business."

WhatsApp Business lets you send messages. Fitosys schedules them, personalises them, tracks who replied, stores the responses, and generates a coaching summary from them every Monday. They are different tools for different jobs.
"I'm not very technical."
Setup takes 30 minutes. There is no code, no integrations to configure, and no ongoing maintenance. If you can share a WhatsApp link, you can use Fitosys.
"I only have 12 clients right now."
That is exactly the right time to set this up — before the manual system breaks. Every coach who waited until they had 30 clients wishes they had started at 12.
"It's
1,499 per month. I need to think about it."
If Fitosys catches one renewal that would have lapsed — one client at
5,000 per month — it has paid for itself for the next 2 to 3 months. The question is not whether
1,499 is worth it. It is how many renewals you are currently losing that you do not know about.
One-Line Descriptions by Context
Context
Line
Instagram bio
Business OS for independent yoga, wellness & nutrition coaches. WhatsApp-native. Built for India.
LinkedIn company page
Fitosys automates client onboarding, weekly check-ins, and renewal reminders for independent coaches in India — natively on WhatsApp.
Google search ad
Stop Losing Clients to Missed Follow-Ups — Fitosys Automates Check-Ins & Renewals on WhatsApp
Referral program
Know a coach spending hours on WhatsApp admin every week? Send them this link. You both get a free month.
LinkedIn post opener
The most common reason clients leave a good coach has nothing to do with the coaching.
11. Typography
Fitosys uses a two-font system designed for maximum impact across digital and print. Unchanged from the original brand document — visual identity carries forward.
Font
Role
Usage
Urbanist
Primary brand font
Body copy, UI text, captions, long-form content, app interfaces. Weights: 300, 400, 600.
Barlow Condensed
Display / hero font
Headlines, section titles, callouts, labels, poster copy. Weights:
. Uppercase preferred.
12. Colour Palette
Unchanged from the original brand document. Visual identity carries forward.
Colour
Hex
Usage Rules
Fitosys Red
Signal colour. Used sparingly for maximum impact. Directs attention. Never use as text on white backgrounds.
Black Core
#0A0A0A
Dominant background. Fitosys lives in the dark. Foundation of all visual layouts.
Surface
Cards, panels, UI elements. Secondary dark layer.
Pure White
#FFFFFF
Primary text on dark backgrounds. Clarity and precision.
Steel Grey
#A0A0A0
Secondary information only. Never for primary communication.
Do not use gradients on the primary red. Keep it flat and confident.
Do not use red text on white backgrounds — it loses the brand's premium feel.
Red is the signal. Use it to direct attention. Use it rarely.
13. Brand Voice & Tone

Fitosys sounds like a sharp, reliable operator who understands the coaching business from the inside. Not a tech startup. Not a fitness brand. A straight-talking product that knows exactly what it does, says so clearly, and never wastes the coach's time with words that do not earn their place.
The three-word voice standard: Clear. Grounded. Precise.
Every piece of Fitosys communication must pass this test before it goes out.
Tone by Context
Context
Tone
What this means in practice
Landing page
Confident and direct
Lead with the problem. Follow with proof. No warm-up paragraphs.
Onboarding emails
Warm and instructional
Coach is new. One clear next step per email. No information overload.
In-product UI copy
Functional and calm
Tell the coach what is happening and what to do next. Nothing more.
WhatsApp to clients
Personal and human
These go out in the coach's name. Must feel like the coach wrote them.
Error states
Honest and calm
State what happened. What Fitosys is doing. What the coach should do if anything.
Support conversations
Patient and specific
Never make a coach feel like they asked a stupid question.
Marketing content
Proof-led and grounded
Real numbers. Real coach problems. Real outcomes. No stock photo energy.
Objection handling
Steady and confident
Never defensive. Never apologetic. Answer directly and move forward.
Writing Rules — Non-Negotiable
Short sentences. One idea per sentence.
Lead with the outcome, then explain how.
Use "you" and "your" directly — always speaking to one coach.
Numbers over adjectives. "2 to 3 hours back every week" over "significant time savings."
Name the specific thing. "Sunday evening check-in messages" over "client communication tasks."
Active voice always. "Fitosys sends the reminder" not "the reminder is sent by Fitosys."
Never hedge the value. "Gets your Sunday evening back" not "may help reduce the time you spend on check-ins."
Words We Use

coaches, clients, system, automated, weekly, renewal, check-in, onboarding, WhatsApp, revenue, time, consistent, reliable, specific, clear, built for, works on, handles, catches, surfaces, sends, tracks
Words We Avoid

transform, journey, ecosystem, supercharge, powerful, seamless, revolutionary, game-changing, crush, hustle, grind, tribe, community (in fitness sense), holistic (unless client-specific), solution (generic), platform (say Fitosys instead)
The Editing Test
Before publishing anything written in the Fitosys voice, check against these four questions:
Could a coach read this in under 30 seconds and know exactly what it means?
Does it name a specific problem or outcome — or does it stay vague?
Does it sound like a person talking to one coach — or a brand talking to a market?
Is every word earning its place — or is something there just to sound complete?
If any answer is no, cut or rewrite before it goes out.
Fitosys is a product of Alchemetryx | fitosys.in | Internal Use Only
